name: Siddhant Verma
bio: I grew up in Piscataway, New Jersey, did my schooling at NPS Yelahanka and college at PES in Electronic City, and somewhere along the way, Bangalore quietly became home.

I'll be honest: I'm the quiet one in most rooms. Small circle, happy to listen. But ask me about motorcycles, or the aviation documentary I watched at 1 a.m., or whatever research paper I accidentally fell into that week, and suddenly I'm the guy who won't stop talking. Consider yourself warned.

Weekends are sacred. They go to my saxophone, a bit of competitive gaming, football with friends, climbing walls, or disappearing on a trek with my camera and coming back with way too many photos. I cook (actually cook, not just survive), I've traded chasing gym numbers for calisthenics, and if there's a shiny new gadget out there, chances are I've already read three reviews and talked myself into wanting it.
summary: I write backend systems for a living, and somewhere along the way ended up doing a lot of the on-call/infrastructure work too. So I keep two versions of my résumé, and which one's relevant depends on what you're hiring for. The backend one is about shipping features end to end: Java, Kotlin, Go, Spring Boot, distributed systems, the kind of work where the win is a number going up on a dashboard. The SRE one is about the 2 a.m. version of me: Kubernetes, GitOps, incident response, keeping the lights on and the AWS bill down. Both describe the same stretch at Sahaj AI, just through a different lens. Grab whichever matches the role, or both for the full picture.

resumes.label: Backend / Product Engineering
resumes.blurb: Feature delivery, distributed systems, revenue-driving work, for backend or product engineering roles.

resumes.label: Site Reliability / Platform Engineering
resumes.blurb: Kubernetes, GitOps, observability, incident response, cost, for SRE or platform roles.

experience.title: Forward Deployed Engineer - 1
narrative: Most of my time here has gone into two very different kinds of work that turned out to be the same job. On the build side: a Kafka pipeline moving 4,000+ telemetry events a second into a state-level dashboard, an equity-management service that handles RSU grants and monetization end-to-end (with a zero-downtime move off QLDB onto Postgres along the way), and a couple of subscription-billing systems for Tata Motors' connected-vehicle fleet that took recurring revenue from ₹5 lakh to ₹1.5 crore a month across roughly 500K vehicles.

On the keep-it-running side, I've been the person who gets paged. I led recovery after a full production EKS cluster outage, including owning the client and developer comms through it, then rebuilt our delivery model onto GitOps (ArgoCD + Crossplane) so the whole environment could be restored from code if it ever happened again. Along the way I got SLA compliance to 99.9%+ across ten services, cut incident-resolution time by about half, and found enough savings from ARM/Graviton migration and cleaning up orphaned cloud resources to take our AWS bill from $3K to $1.2K a month.
bullets: Built and shipped 2 subscription-renewal features end-to-end with Claude in Java/Spring Boot on Tata Motors' Connected Vehicle Platform, helping grow subscription revenue from ₹5L to ₹1.5 Cr/month across ~500K vehicles.
Built a Claude plugin specific to project requirements and service-catalog enabling developers to work with AI.

Designed and built a Kafka-backed telemetry pipeline (4K+ TPS) ingesting multi-channel data through ETL into a warehouse powering state dashboards, plus an ML service reading meter values from operator photos over tiered S3 + Glacier warehouse.

Closed an EV battery-efficiency gap affecting ~1 in 4 telemetry packets by replacing a hardcoded 0.95 constant with a model accounting for regenerative braking, correcting retarding-force and charging calculations.
Owned end-to-end backend delivery of RSUs in Kotlin/Spring Boot: zero-downtime QLDB→PostgreSQL migration, SQS-driven async letter generation, and PII data tokenization.

Led end-to-end recovery of a full production EKS cluster outage, owning all client and developer communications throughout; drove SLA compliance to 99.9%+ across 10 services while cutting incident-resolution time ~50% by rebuilding alerting, runbook coverage, and postmortem culture for a 70+ engineer org.

Migrated cluster delivery from Rancher to a GitOps model (ArgoCD + Crossplane) on EKS, enabling zero-downtime deployments and reproducible infrastructure; rebuilt and restored the entire environment from code after an accidental infrastructure deletion.
Cut monthly AWS spend from $3,000 to $1,200 by migrating builds to ARM/Graviton, reclaiming orphaned NAT gateways and EC2 resources via Terraform and scripts, and automating off-peak scale-down.
Designed a GDPR-compliant PII tokenization scheme (salted/peppered hashing with distributed key division and randomized generation), enabling secure storage and controlled runtime detokenization.

experience.title: Software Development Engineer Intern
narrative: Before I was full-time, I built the infrastructure that let the ML team demo models without babysitting servers: each model upload spun up an isolated, GPU-capable environment on its own, automatically, via Terraform and ECS Fargate, and it tore itself down after 30 minutes of inactivity so we weren't paying for idle GPUs. Extended the same pipeline to real GPU workloads on EC2 g4dn instances so the environment matched whatever the model actually needed.
bullets: Built on-demand infrastructure for ML model demos using Terraform, ECS Fargate, and GitHub Actions: each model upload triggered automated provisioning of an isolated environment with a public-facing domain, supporting ~5 deployments per week with automated teardown eliminating idle GPU costs.
Extended the pipeline for GPU workloads by dynamically provisioning ECS on EC2 GPU instances (g4dn), enabling compute-appropriate environments without manual intervention.

projects.name: Resilient Distributed Key-Value Store
projects.description: 12K ops/sec at P99 80–100ms; built in Go with gRPC/Protobuf, leader-based replication, and consistent hashing.

education.degree: B.Tech. Computer Science and Engineering (Machine Learning and Data Science)
education.detail: C.N.R Rao Merit Scholarship, top 20% by SGPA, 40% tuition refund (Sem 7 & 8).

awards: Runner-up, Hacktoberfest 2021 (PESU Hackerspace): built an education-gamification platform.
publications: Second-hand price prediction, a 93% accuracy end-to-end ML pipeline, published as an IEEE paper.
